_readme: Copy this file to answers.json and adapt it to YOUR situation (answers.json is git-ignored). Match questions by MEANING, not exact wording. PRECEDENCE: before using any 'confirmed' match, scan 'rules' for an entry that scopes or overrides it - rules ALWAYS win over confirmed. 'confirmed' holds atomic, paste-safe answers - used exactly as written. 'rules' holds decision rules - the agent RESOLVES the rule to a single value first and enters only that value; rule text is NEVER pasted into a form. If a question is not covered here or in profile.json, the agent STOPS and asks the user, then saves it: context-free facts about the user go to 'confirmed' phrased generically; anything conditional on the job (hours, location, stack, salary, start date) goes to 'rules' with its scope stated. When an existing answer's scope doesn't match the current job, the agent re-confirms instead of reusing it.

rules.Work authorization / visa sponsorship: NEVER claim authorization anywhere except the countries listed in profile.json legal_authorization. (a) Question names a specific country you are NOT authorized in ('Are you authorized to work in the US?') → answer 'No', regardless of remote status. (b) Sponsorship question: remote role workable from your country → 'No' (add 'I will work remotely from [your country] - no sponsorship needed' in any free-text/comment field); role requiring relocation → 'Yes'. (c) Job in your authorized country → authorized 'Yes', sponsorship 'No'. (d) Posting unclear about remote eligibility → STOP and ask the user before answering.
rules.Salary expectation: Read the field's label for period and currency: monthly numeric field → monthly amount from profile.json; annual numeric field → yearly_equivalent. If the field expects another currency or the period is unclear → STOP and ask the user. Optional/free-text salary field → write 'Negotiable'.
rules.Notice period / earliest start date: Free-text or dropdown → the notice_period value from profile.json. Date-picker/date input → enter the concrete date (today's date if immediately available).
rules.Have you ever worked for this company be: Check profile.json experience[] company names first. Applying to a past employer or its subsidiaries → 'Yes' and describe the role. Any other company → 'No'.
rules.How did you hear about us?: Only claim a source if it is actually where this posting came from; if the source of the current URL is unknown, ask the user (one word). If the true source is not an option, pick the truthful closest ('Job board', 'Other'). Never claim an untrue source (e.g. 'Employee referral').
rules.Years of PROFESSIONAL / paid / full-time: OVERRIDES any confirmed years-of-experience numbers, which may include personal projects. If a question explicitly says 'professional', 'paid', or 'full-time' experience, do not reuse them - STOP and ask the user how to answer.
rules.Consent checkboxes: Required privacy-policy / data-processing consent (needed to submit) → check it. Optional marketing emails, newsletters, or talent-pool retention → leave unchecked and mention it in the handover report.

needs_confirmation._note: New unknown questions go to the user live during an application, then get saved to 'confirmed' or 'rules' per the _readme.
